# Custom Context System

This directory allows you to customize AI agent behavior at multiple levels.

**Note**: These files are synced from the [agent-rules repository](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules) via `agent-sync`. To contribute improvements to the shared rules, skills, or this documentation, submit a PR to that repository.

## Agent Context Hierarchy

AI agents read context in this priority order:

| Level | Purpose | Distribution |
| ----- | ------- | ------------ |
| [1. OS](#1-os)<br>`~/AGENTS.md` → tool symlinks* | Broad guidance: code quality, commit style, general preferences (applies to all repos on your machine) | Via setup script, or manually |
| [2. All Repos](#2-all-repos)<br>[`<repo>/AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md) | **Entry point**: organization-wide shared standards; pulls in all `*_RULES.md` files via `@` file includes | Via [agent-sync](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules/blob/main/bin/agent-sync) |
| [3. Repo-Specific](#3-repo-specific)<br>`<repo>/.agents/REPO_RULES.md` | Repo gotchas, conventions, patterns | Via [agent-sync](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules/blob/main/bin/agent-sync) |
| [4. Subdirectory](#4-subdirectory)<br>`<repo>/<subdir>/AGENTS.md` | Language or framework-specific rules scoped to a subtree; closest file wins | Manual (can be added to git) |
| [5. Team](#5-team)<br>`<repo>/.agents/TEAM_RULES.md` | Team conventions, shared offline | Gitignored — manual |
| [6. Personal](#6-personal)<br>`<repo>/.agents/USER_RULES.md` | Your personal, repo-specific preferences, workflow style | Gitignored — manual |
| [7. Memory](#7-memory)<br>`<repo>/.agents/LEARNING_LOG.md` | Agent-written log of discovered patterns; reduces hallucinations over time | Gitignored — manual |

*See [1. OS](#1-os) section below for symlink details.

**Key principle**: Higher levels take precedence; last file read wins on conflicts. Lower levels are additive and supplement higher levels.

## Customization Layers

### 1. OS

**File:** `~/AGENTS.md` → tool symlinks*

For rules that apply to all your projects, use OS-level configuration. The setup script creates `~/AGENTS.md` as the source of truth and symlinks so each tool finds it at its expected path.

Use for: coding style, commit message format, general preferences that apply across all your projects.

**What the setup script creates**:

- `~/AGENTS.md` — source of truth (created if missing)
- `~/.agents/skills` and `~/.agents/commands` — shared skill/command directories
- `~/CLAUDE.md` and `~/CODEX.md` — symlinks to `~/AGENTS.md` (Claude and Codex read from home)
- `~/.claude/skills`, `~/.claude/commands`, `~/.codex/skills`, `~/.codex/commands` — symlinks to `~/.agents/` counterparts

Gemini uses `~/AGENTS.md` and `~/.agents` directly, so no extra symlinks are needed.

Unfortunately, Cursor does not support OS-level file-based setup; it only supports repository-level. For OS-level Cursor rules, manually add them via Cursor Settings → Rules → User Rules.

**Setup**:

1. Clone [agent-rules](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules) (if not already):

   ```bash
   cd ~/invoca
   git clone https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules.git
   ```

2. Run [local-os-agent-setup.sh](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules/blob/main/bin/local-os-agent-setup.sh) to create `~/AGENTS.md`, subdirectories, and symlinks:

   ```bash
   ~/invoca/agent-rules/bin/local-os-agent-setup.sh
   ```

---

### 2. All Repos

**File:** [`<repo>/AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md)

Entry point for all agent context. Shared across all Invoca repos via [agent-sync](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules/blob/main/bin/agent-sync) and version-controlled. Contains organization-wide standards and pulls in all `*_RULES.md` files beneath it in the hierarchy via `@` file includes.

Use for: organization-wide coding standards, shared team conventions, repo architecture patterns common to all Invoca repos.

---

### 3. Repo-Specific

**File:** `<repo>/.agents/REPO_RULES.md`

Created automatically by [agent-sync](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules/blob/main/bin/agent-sync) and version-controlled. Add repository-specific conventions, architecture notes, and known gotchas here. Shared across the team via agent-sync.

Use for: repo-specific architecture decisions, naming conventions, known constraints, and gotchas unique to this codebase.

---

### 4. Subdirectory

**File:** `<repo>/<subdir>/AGENTS.md`

Organize rules by placing `AGENTS.md` files throughout your project. Nested rules automatically attach when files in their directory are referenced. Created manually and can be added to git.

```text
project/
  AGENTS.md              # Project-wide rules
  backend/
    server/
      AGENTS.md          # Backend-specific rules
  frontend/
    AGENTS.md            # Frontend-specific rules
```

- The **closest** `AGENTS.md` to the edited file wins (like CSS specificity)
- Subproject rules **extend**, not replace, the root rules
- Works across Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, and [60k+ open-source projects](https://agents.md)

See [Language and Framework Specific Rules](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules/tree/main/.agents) for examples you can drop into your subfolders.

Use for: language or framework-specific rules, patterns scoped to a subtree (e.g., frontend conventions, backend service rules).

---

### 5. Team

**File:** `<repo>/.agents/TEAM_RULES.md` _(gitignored — manual)_

Team conventions shared offline:

```bash
cp .agents/TEAM_RULES.md.example .agents/TEAM_RULES.md
```

See [TEAM_RULES.md.example](TEAM_RULES.md.example) for a starter template.

Use for: code review guidelines, testing standards, team workflows.

---

### 6. Personal

**File:** `<repo>/.agents/USER_RULES.md` _(gitignored — manual)_

Personal AI customization:

```bash
cp .agents/USER_RULES.md.example .agents/USER_RULES.md
```

See [USER_RULES.md.example](USER_RULES.md.example) for a starter template.

Use for: coding style, workflow preferences, experiments, personal reminders.

---

### 7. Memory

**File:** `<repo>/.agents/LEARNING_LOG.md` _(gitignored — manual)_

Use for: persistent agent memory. Patterns discovered during sessions, corrections received, codebase quirks. Grows automatically.

Agent-writable log of discovered patterns (opt-in). The agent reads it at session start and appends to it over time, building up repo-specific memory so you don't have to re-explain the same context every session.

Because it's a plain markdown file in a standard location, it works as a shared memory layer across all agents: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini all read the same file. A pattern the agent learns in one tool is immediately available in the next. No per-tool configuration, no repeated onboarding. Just a log that gets smarter with use.

To try it out:

```bash
cp .agents/LEARNING_LOG.md.example .agents/LEARNING_LOG.md
```

See [LEARNING_LOG.md.example](LEARNING_LOG.md.example) for the log format and maintenance tips.

## Commands and Skills

In addition to the rules-based context files above, agents support reusable **commands** (slash-command workflows) and **skills** (specialized capability modules).

### Shared Commands and Skills (from agent-rules)

Shared commands live in `<repo>/.agents/commands/` and skills in `<repo>/.agents/skills/`. These are managed by [agent-sync](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules/blob/main/bin/agent-sync) and should not be edited directly — changes will be overwritten on the next sync.

You can identify auto-generated files by their markers:

- **Commands** include this header:

  ```markdown
  <!-- AUTO-GENERATED by agent-sync — do not edit manually. -->
  <!-- https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules -->
  ```

- **Skills** include this front-matter:

  ```yaml
  source: agent-rules
  read-only: true
  ```

To improve a shared command or skill, submit a PR to [agent-rules](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules).

### Repo-Specific Commands and Skills

You can safely add your own repo-specific commands and skills alongside the shared ones — **agent-sync will never overwrite them** because they lack the auto-generated markers.

| Type | Location |
| ---- | -------- |
| Repo command | `<repo>/.agents/commands/MY-COMMAND.md` |
| Repo skill | `<repo>/.agents/skills/<my-skill>/SKILL.md` |

Repo-specific commands and skills are committed to your repo like any other file and evolve independently from the shared agent-rules ones.

Use repo commands for workflows specific to your codebase (e.g., running your test suite, deploying to staging). Use repo skills for domain knowledge or capabilities unique to your application.

---

## Contributing

Found a useful pattern that should be shared?

**Workflow:**

1. **Experiment** locally in `USER_RULES.md` or `TEAM_RULES.md`
2. **Validate** it works in real usage over several sessions
3. **Upstream** via PR:
   - **Repo-specific patterns** → Add to this repo's `.agents/REPO_RULES.md`
   - **Broadly useful patterns** → Submit PR to [agent-rules](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules) to add to `AGENTS.md`
4. **Benefit everyone** - Your improvement syncs to all Invoca repos via `agent-sync`

See the [agent-rules README](https://github.com/Invoca/agent-rules#readme) for contribution guidelines.
